title: Dutch company ASML is $300bn from a trillion. AI could close the gap
excerpt: ASML has spent 2026 getting steadily, expensively larger. The Dutch company whose machines print the world’s most advanced chips is now Europe’s most valuable listed business, worth around $700bn (€600bn), and enough of the investors who own it now say the next round number out loud that the question has stopped sounding fanciful: could ASML […]
content_html:
ASML has spent 2026 getting steadily, expensively larger. The Dutch company whose machines print the world’s most advanced chips is now Europe’s most valuable listed business, worth around $700bn (€600bn), and enough of the investors who own it now say the next round number out loud that the question has stopped sounding fanciful: could ASML be Europe’s first trillion-dollar company?

The case rests on the order book more than the share price. AI has pushed demand for lithography systems past what ASML can build, and the company is shaving weeks off the time it takes to assemble each machine, from about 22 weeks toward 15 or 16.

While planning to lift annual output of its extreme ultraviolet systems by around 30% next year and to raise production of its cheaper deep-ultraviolet machines by a similar margin. It has raised its 2026 sales forecast twice and is close to fully booked for 2027.

The maths is not exotic. Reaching a trillion means adding roughly $300bn, a gain of a little over 40% on a stock that has already climbed about 60% this year and, in June, became the most valuable European company on record.

ASML has added well over $250bn in market value this year alone, and Barclays, Susquehanna and Bernstein have all nudged price targets toward $2,600 a share since the results, even with the stock trading near 38 times next year’s expected earnings.

“I think it has a really good chance of being the first company in Europe to hit the trillion mark,” Carolyn Bell of Stonehage Fleming said. “I just don’t know when.”

The second-quarter figures gave the argument its legs. ASML reported net sales of €9.3bn and net income of €2.9bn at a 54% gross margin, shipped 86 lithography systems against 67 in the first quarter, and lifted full-year guidance to €43-45bn from a previous €36-40bn.

It guided to €11-12bn of net sales in the third quarter and returned €1.1bn to shareholders through buybacks in the period, and chief executive Christophe Fouquet put the momentum down to customers accelerating capacity for AI.

Underneath the numbers is a monopoly. ASML is the only firm that makes EUV lithography machines, and this year Intel became the first to ship high-volume commercial chips, its Panther Lake laptop processors, made on the newest High-NA version, a $400m tool roughly twice the price of a standard EUV system.

High-NA machines resolve finer features in a single exposure, which is what lets chipmakers keep shrinking the transistors that AI accelerators are hungry for.

TSMC and Samsung are following at their own pace, which means every leading-edge chipmaker eventually pays ASML.

The caveats are the familiar ones. Roughly a fifth of this year’s sales are expected to come from China, down sharply from around half two years ago as export controls bite, and a proposed US law could narrow that channel further.

The broader worry is cyclicality, since lithography orders track the capital spending of a handful of chipmakers and the hyperscalers funding them, and that spending has turned down before.

Shares actually fell on the day of the guidance upgrade, a reminder that a stock priced for perfection can disappoint by merely doing well. What steadies the story is visibility: ASML books its machines years before it ships them, so the near-term revenue is easier to see than for most hardware companies.

The strategic picture is steadier than the share price, which is why the Dutch government has gone to some lengths to keep the company at home.

The title of Europe’s most valuable company has changed hands more than once in recent years, passing between software, luxury and pharma, and never settling for long.

This time a chipmaker may be the one to push the continent past a marker only a handful of American technology giants have ever reached.

sentences.zh: 这些警告是熟悉的。

sentences.en: Roughly a fifth of this year’s sales are expected to come from China, down sharply from around half two years ago as export controls bite, and a proposed US law could narrow that channel further.

blocks.html_en: The broader worry is cyclicality, since lithography orders track the capital spending of a handful of chipmakers and the hyperscalers funding them, and that spending has turned down before.

sentences.en: The broader worry is cyclicality, since lithography orders track the capital spending of a handful of chipmakers and the hyperscalers funding them, and that spending has turned down before.

What steadies the story is visibility: ASML books its machines years before it ships them, so the near-term revenue is easier to see than for most hardware companies.

sentences.en: Shares actually fell on the day of the guidance upgrade, a reminder that a stock priced for perfection can disappoint by merely doing well.
sentences.zh: 事实上，股价在指引升级当天下跌，这提醒人们，一只定价完美的股票可能会因为表现良好而令人失望。

sentences.en: What steadies the story is visibility: ASML books its machines years before it ships them, so the near-term revenue is easier to see than for most hardware companies.
sentences.zh: 稳定这个故事的是可见性：ASML 在发货前几年就预订了机器，因此短期收入比大多数硬件公司更容易看到。

blocks.html_en: The strategic picture is steadier than the share price, which is why the Dutch government has gone to some lengths to keep the company at home.

sentences.en: The strategic picture is steadier than the share price, which is why the Dutch government has gone to some lengths to keep the company at home.
sentences.zh: 战略前景比股价更稳定，这就是荷兰政府竭尽全力将该公司留在国内的原因。

blocks.html_en: The title of Europe’s most valuable company has changed hands more than once in recent years, passing between software, luxury and pharma, and never settling for long.

sentences.en: The title of Europe’s most valuable company has changed hands more than once in recent years, passing between software, luxury and pharma, and never settling for long.
sentences.zh: 近年来，欧洲最有价值公司的头衔不止一次易手，在软件、奢侈品和制药之间交替，而且从未长期稳定下来。

blocks.html_en: This time a chipmaker may be the one to push the continent past a marker only a handful of American technology giants have ever reached.

sentences.en: This time a chipmaker may be the one to push the continent past a marker only a handful of American technology giants have ever reached.
sentences.zh: 这一次，一家芯片制造商可能会推动非洲大陆超越只有少数美国科技巨头才能达到的水平。

content_html_zh:
ASML 在 2026 年稳步、昂贵地扩大规模。 这家使用机器打印世界上最先进芯片的荷兰公司现在是欧洲最有价值的上市企业，价值约 7000 亿美元（6000 亿欧元），拥有该公司的投资者现在已经有足够多的人大声说出下一轮数字，这个问题听起来不再是异想天开：ASML 会成为欧洲第一家价值数万亿美元的公司吗？

案件更多地取决于订单簿而不是股价。 人工智能已经推动光刻系统的需求超出了 ASML 的制造能力，该公司正在将每台机器的组装时间缩短数周，从大约 22 周缩短到 15 或 16 周。

同时计划明年将其极紫外线系统的年产量提高约 30%，并以类似的幅度提高更便宜的深紫外线机器的产量。 该公司已两次上调 2026 年的销售预测，2027 年的销售预测已接近售罄。

数学并不陌生。 达到一万亿美元意味着增加约 3000 亿美元，相对于今年已上涨约 60% 并在 6 月成为有记录以来最有价值的欧洲公司的股票而言，涨幅略高于 40%。

仅今年一年，ASML 的市值就增加了超过 2500 亿美元，巴克莱银行 (Barclays)、萨斯奎哈纳银行 (Susquehanna) 和伯恩斯坦银行 (Bernstein) 自公布结果以来都将目标股价推向每股 2,600 美元，尽管该股的交易价格接近明年预期收益的 38 倍。

Stonehage Fleming 的卡罗琳·贝尔 (Carolyn Bell) 表示：“我认为它很有可能成为欧洲第一家突破万亿美元大关的公司。” “我只是不知道什么时候。”

第二季度的数据为这一争论提供了依据。 ASML 报告净销售额为 93 亿欧元，净利润为 29 亿欧元，毛利率为 54%，第一季度光刻系统出货量为 67 套，而出货量为 86 套，并将全年指引从之前的 36-400 亿欧元上调至 43-450 亿欧元。

该公司第三季度净销售额为 11-120 亿欧元，并通过回购向股东返还 11 亿欧元，首席执行官 Christophe Fouquet 将这一势头归因于客户加速人工智能产能。

数字的背后是垄断。 ASML 是唯一一家生产 EUV 光刻机的公司，今年英特尔成为第一家大批量生产商用芯片的公司，其 Panther Lake 笔记本电脑处理器采用最新的 High-NA 版本制造，这是一款价值 4 亿美元的工具，大约是标准 EUV 系统价格的两倍。

高数值孔径机器可以在一次曝光中解决更精细的特征，这使得芯片制造商能够不断缩小人工智能加速器所渴望的晶体管。

台积电和三星正在按照自己的步调紧随其后，这意味着每家领先的芯片制造商最终都会向 ASML 付款。

这些警告是熟悉的。 由于出口管制的影响，今年销售额的大约五分之一预计将来自中国，这一数字比两年前的约一半大幅下降，而拟议的美国法律可能会进一步缩小这一渠道。

更广泛的担忧是周期性，因为光刻订单跟踪少数芯片制造商和为其提供资金的超大规模企业的资本支出，而这种支出之前已经下降。

事实上，股价在指引升级当天下跌，这提醒人们，一只定价完美的股票可能会因为表现良好而令人失望。 稳定这个故事的是可见性：ASML 在发货前几年就预订了机器，因此短期收入比大多数硬件公司更容易看到。

战略前景比股价更稳定，这就是荷兰政府竭尽全力将该公司留在国内的原因。

近年来，欧洲最有价值公司的头衔不止一次易手，在软件、奢侈品和制药之间交替，而且从未长期稳定下来。

这一次，一家芯片制造商可能会推动非洲大陆超越只有少数美国科技巨头才能达到的水平。
